prompt:
Our audit-log export endpoint streams NDJSON over chunked HTTP/1.1 — chunkstream.ts writes straight to the connection so we never buffer a whole export. Two kinds of tickets since the compliance dashboards moved onto it, and I've stared at this code way too long.

One: some exports just stop partway. The download 'completes' as far as curl is concerned, but the last chunk of records is missing. It's always the SAME exports that truncate — retries change nothing — while both tiny and huge exports are usually fine, so it isn't a size cap or a timeout. Diffing two exports that differ by one record: one is complete, the other loses everything after a certain line. Cannot see a pattern.

Two: when the upstream log reader errors mid-export, our API docs promise the client gets a trailing {"error": ...} record and a clean end of body. What clients actually see is literal 'HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error' text spliced INTO the middle of the download, and strict clients then scream about malformed chunked encoding. When the reader errors before any output, though, the 500 comes out perfectly — which makes even less sense to me.

A teammate turned captured connection bytes into test_stream.ts using a fake socket, so the whole mess reproduces deterministically: node --test test_stream.ts fails today exactly the way production does (and it re-checks the exports that DO work, which must keep working). Find what's wrong in chunkstream.ts and fix it; the test file is read-only, and don't 'fix' it by buffering whole exports in memory — streaming stays.
